title: lophochloa cristata var. pumila
intro: Lophochloa cristata var. pumila, also known as Dwarf Lophochloa, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Lophochloa cristata var. pumila include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely documented in modern medicine, and further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
